exampleEn: Radioactive isotope of certain chemical elements such as carbon-14 and uranium-235 have unstable atomic nuclei that spontaneously decay over predictable periods of time known as their characteristic half-lives transforming into more stable daughter nuclei while simultaneously emitting detectable subatomic particles and electromagnetic radiation in the process which scientists can measure and use for various practical applications including archaeological dating and medical diagnostic imaging of internal body structures.
exampleZh: 某些 chemical elements 如 carbon-14 和 uranium-235 的 radioactive isotope 具有 unstable atomic nuclei 这些 nuclei spontaneously decay over predictable periods of time known as their characteristic half-lives  transforming into more stable daughter nuclei 同时 simultaneously emitting detectable subatomic particles and electromagnetic radiation in the process 科学家可以 measure 并用于 various practical applications including archaeological dating and medical diagnostic imaging of internal body structures

exampleEn: The kinetic theory of gases explains the macroscopic observable properties of gases such as pressure temperature and volume in terms of the collective average behavior of enormous numbers of microscopic individual gas molecules in constant rapid random motion colliding elastically with one another and with the containing walls of their enclosure transferring momentum and energy back and forth in countless billions of individual molecular-scale interactions occurring every single second throughout the entire volume of the gas sample.
exampleZh: kinetic theory of gases 根据 enormous numbers of microscopic individual gas molecules in constant rapid random motion colliding elastically with one another and with the containing walls of their enclosure 的 collective average behavior 解释气体的 macroscopic observable properties 如 pressure temperature and volume 在 the entire volume of the gas sample 中每秒钟发生 countless billions of individual molecular-scale interactions transferring momentum and energy back and forth

exampleEn: Laser technology produces an intense highly focused beam of coherent light in which all the individual light waves oscillate in perfect synchrony with one another at exactly the same precise wavelength and phase enabling applications that require extreme precision and concentrated optical power delivery such as delicate eye microsurgery high-speed fiber-optic telecommunications and the precise cutting and welding of thick steel plates in heavy industrial manufacturing processes worldwide.
exampleZh: laser 技术产生 intense highly focused beam of coherent light 其中所有 individual light waves 以 exactly the same precise wavelength and phase oscillate in perfect synchrony with one another enabling 需要 extreme precision and concentrated optical power delivery 的 applications 如 delicate eye microsurgery high-speed fiber-optic telecommunications and the precise cutting and welding of thick steel plates in heavy industrial manufacturing processes worldwide

exampleEn: The compound optical microscope uses a carefully calibrated system of multiple precision-ground glass lens arranged in a specific configuration along the vertical optical axis to produce highly magnified clear images of extremely tiny specimens such as individual living cells and subcellular organelles that would otherwise remain completely invisible and undetectable to the unaided naked human eye alone without the assistance of such sophisticated optical magnification instrumentation and technology.
exampleZh: Compound optical microscope 使用 a carefully calibrated system of multiple precision-ground glass lens arranged in a specific configuration along the vertical optical axis 以 produce highly magnified clear images of extremely tiny specimens such as individual living cells and subcellular organelles 否则这些 specimen 将 remain completely invisible and undetectable to the unaided naked human eye alone without the assistance of such sophisticated optical magnification instrumentation and technology

exampleEn: A lever is one of the six fundamental classical simple machines identified by ancient Greek engineers and mathematicians that amplifies an input effort force applied at one point along a rigid beam rotating around a fixed pivot point called the fulcrum to produce a larger output force capable of lifting or moving a heavy load object positioned at a different location along the same beam with much less muscular effort required from the human operator performing the lifting or moving task.
exampleZh: lever 是 ancient Greek engineers and mathematicians identified 的 six fundamental classical simple machines 之一 它 amplifies an input effort force applied at one point along a rigid beam rotating around a fixed pivot point called the fulcrum 以 produce a larger output force capable of lifting or moving a heavy load object positioned at a different location along the same beam 而 human operator performing the lifting or moving task 所需的 muscular effort 大大减少

exampleEn: A permanent magnet creates an invisible three-dimensional magnetic field in the space surrounding it that can attract or repel certain other magnetic materials such as those containing the ferromagnetic elements iron nickel and cobalt without requiring any direct physical contact between the magnet and the distant object being acted upon through the empty space separating them sometimes across surprisingly large distances relative to the physical size of the magnet itself.
exampleZh: Permanent magnet 在其周围的 space 中 creates an invisible three-dimensional magnetic field 可以 attract or repel 某些其他 magnetic materials 例如那些含有 ferromagnetic elements iron nickel and cobalt 的材料 而无需 magnet 和 being acted upon through the empty space separating them 的 distant object 之间的 any direct physical contact 有时跨越 surprisingly large distances relative to the physical size of the magnet itself

exampleEn: In physics mass is a fundamental intrinsic property of all matter that measures an object's inherent resistance to being accelerated when a net external force is applied to it a concept known as inertia and is quite distinct from the related but separate concept of weight which depends on the local strength of the gravitational field in which the object happens to be located at any given moment in time and space.
exampleZh: 在物理学中 mass 是所有物质的一个 fundamental intrinsic property 它 measures an object's inherent resistance to being accelerated when a net external force is applied to it 这个概念称为 inertia 与 related but separate concept of weight  quite distinct 后者取决于 object 在任何 given moment in time and space happens to be located 的 gravitational field 的 local strength

exampleEn: All ordinary matter in the observable universe that we can directly detect and study with our scientific instruments is composed of atoms which are themselves built from just three types of fundamental subatomic particles namely positively charged protons and electrically neutral neutrons tightly bound together in the dense central atomic nucleus surrounded by a diffuse cloud of much lighter negatively charged electrons orbiting at various discrete distances and energy levels around that central nuclear core region.
exampleZh: Observable universe 中我们可以 directly detect and study with our scientific instruments 的所有 ordinary matter 都是由 atoms composed 的 而 atoms 本身又由 just three types of fundamental subatomic particles built 即 positively charged protons 和 electrically neutral neutrons tightly bound together 在 dense central atomic nucleus 中 周围环绕着 much lighter negatively charged electrons 的 diffuse cloud orbiting at various discrete distances and energy levels around that central nuclear core region

exampleEn: The melting point of a pure crystalline solid substance is a characteristic physical property that can be used to help identify an unknown chemical compound because different pure substances melt at sharply defined and highly reproducible temperatures under standard atmospheric pressure conditions at sea level whereas impure mixtures typically melt over a broader and less well-defined temperature range that begins at a lower temperature than the pure substance alone would exhibit under otherwise identical heating and environmental measurement conditions.
exampleZh: Pure crystalline solid substance 的 melting point 是一个 characteristic physical property 可用于帮助 identify an unknown chemical compound 因为不同的 pure substances 在 standard atmospheric pressure conditions at sea level 下 melt at sharply defined and highly reproducible temperatures 而 impure mixtures 通常 melt over a broader and less well-defined temperature range 该范围始于比 pure substance alone would exhibit under otherwise identical heating and environmental measurement conditions 更低的温度

exampleEn: When a small rocky or metallic fragment of space debris enters the Earth's upper atmosphere at extremely high velocities typically exceeding twenty kilometers per second the intense friction with air molecules causes it to heat up so rapidly and intensely that it becomes a glowing streak of incandescent white light racing across the night sky a phenomenon commonly called a shooting star or more scientifically accurately termed a meteor by professional astronomers and planetary scientists studying near-Earth objects and solar system debris.
exampleZh: 当 a small rocky or metallic fragment of space debris 以 extremely high velocities typically exceeding twenty kilometers per second enters the Earth's upper atmosphere 与 air molecules 的 intense friction 使其 so rapidly and intensely heat up 以至于它 becomes a glowing streak of incandescent white light racing across the night sky 这种现象通常称为 shooting star 或更 scientifically accurately termed a meteor by professional astronomers and planetary scientists studying near-Earth objects and solar system debris

exampleEn: Not all microbe that exist in the natural environment are harmful to humans in fact the vast majority of bacterial and fungal species living freely in soil water and even on and within our own bodies play essential beneficial roles in maintaining the health and proper functioning of ecosystems and individual organisms through processes such as nutrient cycling decomposition of organic waste materials and protection against colonization by genuinely pathogenic disease-causing microorganisms.
exampleZh: 并非 natural environment 中存在的所有 microbe 都对人类 harmful 事实上绝大多数生活在 soil water 甚至我们自身 bodies on and within 的 bacterial and fungal species 通过 nutrient cycling decomposition of organic waste materials and protection against colonization by genuinely pathogenic disease-causing microorganisms 等过程 play essential beneficial roles in maintaining the health and proper functioning of ecosystems and individual organisms

exampleEn: The electron microscope can magnify biological specimens up to two million times their actual physical size revealing the intricate internal structural details of individual organelles within living cells and even the precise spatial arrangement of individual atoms within crystalline materials at a level of resolution that would have seemed like pure science fiction fantasy to scientists working just a single generation ago before these remarkable technological advances became widely available in research laboratories worldwide.
exampleZh: Electron microscope 可以将 biological specimens magnify up to two million times their actual physical size  revealing the intricate internal structural details of individual organelles within living cells 甚至 the precise spatial arrangement of individual atoms within crystalline materials 其 resolution 水平在仅仅 a single generation ago 在 these remarkable technological advances became widely available in research laboratories worldwide 之前对 scientists 来说 would have seemed like pure science fiction fantasy

exampleEn: Geologists classify mineral as naturally occurring inorganic solid substances with a specific and well-defined repeating internal crystalline atomic structure and a characteristic chemical composition or range of compositions that can be expressed by a standardized chemical formula such as silicon dioxide for the common rock-forming mineral quartz found abundantly in continental crustal rocks across every major tectonic plate on the surface of our planet Earth.
exampleZh: Geologists classify mineral 为 naturally occurring inorganic solid substances 具有 specific and well-defined repeating internal crystalline atomic structure 和 characteristic chemical composition or range of compositions that can be expressed by a standardized chemical formula 例如 common rock-forming mineral quartz 的 silicon dioxide  found abundantly in continental crustal rocks across every major tectonic plate on the surface of our planet Earth

exampleEn: The principle of conservation of linear momentum states that the total momentum of an isolated system of interacting particles or objects remains absolutely constant over time provided that no net external forces from outside the defined system boundaries act upon any of the component particles or objects within it which is why when a moving billiard ball strikes a stationary one on a smooth level table the first ball slows down while the second ball begins moving in the same original direction with approximately the same speed that the first ball initially possessed before the collision occurred.
exampleZh: Principle of conservation of linear momentum 指出 只要 no net external forces from outside the defined system boundaries act upon any of the component particles or objects within it 一个由 interacting particles or objects 组成的 isolated system 的 total momentum  over time remains absolutely constant 这就是为什么当 moving billiard ball 在 smooth level table 上 strikes a stationary one 时 first ball slows down 而 second ball begins moving in the same original direction 其速度 approximately the same speed that the first ball initially possessed before the collision occurred

exampleEn: A random genetic mutation occurring in the DNA sequence of a single reproductive cell can sometimes produce a novel inherited trait that confers even a very slight survival or reproductive advantage to the individual organism carrying that particular altered gene variant compared to other members of its species lacking the mutation which over many successive generations of natural selection can gradually increase the frequency of that originally rare beneficial genetic variant throughout the entire interbreeding population in a particular local environmental setting and ecological context.
exampleZh: 发生在 a single reproductive cell 的 DNA sequence 中的 random genetic mutation 有时可以 produce a novel inherited trait that confers even a very slight survival or reproductive advantage to the individual organism carrying that particular altered gene variant 与 lacking the mutation 的其 species 的 other members 相比 经过 natural selection 的 many successive generations 可以 gradually increase the frequency of that originally rare beneficial genetic variant throughout the entire interbreeding population in a particular local environmental setting and ecological context

exampleEn: The atomic nucleus of most common chemical elements contains both positively charged protons and electrically neutral neutron tightly bound together by the extremely powerful but very short-range strong nuclear fundamental force which overcomes the intense mutual electrostatic repulsion between the crowded positively charged protons confined within the incredibly tiny volume of the dense central nucleus at the very heart of every single atom in the physical universe around us.
exampleZh: 大多数常见 chemical elements 的 atomic nucleus 包含 positively charged protons 和 electrically neutral neutron 由 extremely powerful but very short-range strong nuclear fundamental force tightly bound together 该力克服了 crowded positively charged protons  confined within the incredibly tiny volume of the dense central nucleus at the very heart of every single atom in the physical universe around us 之间的 intense mutual electrostatic repulsion

exampleEn: The dense central nucleus of an atom which contains over ninety-nine point nine percent of the atom's total mass but occupies only about one trillionth of its total physical volume is composed of positively charged protons and uncharged neutrons held together by the strong nuclear force and surrounded at relatively enormous distances by a diffuse cloud of much lighter negatively charged electrons orbiting in specific quantized energy shells around that incredibly tiny and unimaginably dense nuclear core region at the very center of every atom that makes up all ordinary matter in our physical world.
exampleZh: Atom 的 dense central nucleus 包含 over ninety-nine point nine percent of the atom's total mass 但仅 occupy about one trillionth of its total physical volume 由 positively charged protons 和 uncharged neutrons composed 由 strong nuclear force held together 并由 much lighter negatively charged electrons 的 diffuse cloud surrounded at relatively enormous distances orbiting in specific quantized energy shells around that incredibly tiny and unimaginably dense nuclear core region at the very center of every atom that makes up all ordinary matter in our physical world

exampleEn: The researchers set up a network of automated motion-sensitive cameras throughout the dense tropical rainforest to observe the natural behavior of elusive nocturnal wildlife species in their undisturbed native habitat without the potentially disturbing and behavior-altering physical presence of human scientific observers nearby that might cause the animals to modify their normal activity patterns and movement routines in response to the perceived foreign intrusion into their familiar home territory.
exampleZh: 研究人员在 dense tropical rainforest 中设置了 automated motion-sensitive cameras 的 network 以 observe elusive nocturnal wildlife species 在它们 undisturbed native habitat 中的 natural behavior 而无需 human scientific observers 的 potentially disturbing and behavior-altering physical presence nearby 这可能导致 animals modify their normal activity patterns and movement routines in response to the perceived foreign intrusion into their familiar home territory

exampleEn: Artificial satellites placed into low Earth orbit at altitudes ranging from approximately two hundred to two thousand kilometers above our planet's surface complete one full revolution around the Earth roughly every ninety minutes traveling at blistering speeds of nearly twenty-eight thousand kilometers per hour to balance the inward pull of Earth's gravitational attraction against their natural tendency to continue moving forward in a straight line through the frictionless vacuum of empty space according to the fundamental physical principles of orbital mechanics first described by Isaac Newton over three centuries ago.
exampleZh: 放置在 altitudes ranging from approximately two hundred to two thousand kilometers above our planet's surface 的 low Earth orbit 中的人造 satellites 大约每九十分钟 complete one full revolution around the Earth 以 nearly twenty-eight thousand kilometers per hour 的 blistering speeds traveling 以 balance the inward pull of Earth's gravitational attraction against their natural tendency to continue moving forward in a straight line through the frictionless vacuum of empty space 根据 Isaac Newton over three centuries ago 首次描述的 orbital mechanics 的 fundamental physical principles

exampleEn: Every living organism from the simplest single-celled bacterium to the largest blue whale that has ever inhabited the Earth's vast oceans relies on the same fundamental set of biochemical processes including cellular respiration protein synthesis and DNA replication to maintain the complex organized state of matter and energy flow that distinguishes living biological entities from the non-living inanimate physical world of rocks minerals water and atmospheric gases that surrounds and sustains them all throughout their finite individual lifespans and across the endless generations of evolutionary time.
exampleZh: 从 simplest single-celled bacterium 到曾经 inhabit 地球广阔海洋的 largest blue whale 每个 living organism 都依赖于同一套 fundamental set of biochemical processes 包括 cellular respiration protein synthesis 和 DNA replication 以 maintain the complex organized state of matter and energy flow that distinguishes living biological entities from the non-living inanimate physical world of rocks minerals water and atmospheric gases that surrounds and sustains them all throughout their finite individual lifespans and across the endless generations of evolutionary time

exampleEn: Iron oxide commonly known as rust forms when metallic iron is exposed to both oxygen and moisture simultaneously over an extended period of time through a slow spontaneous electrochemical corrosion process that gradually and relentlessly converts the strong silvery-gray elemental metal into a weak flaky reddish-brown powdery substance that eventually crumbles away to nothing if the destructive corrosive chemical reaction is allowed to continue unchecked and unprotected against the surrounding environmental elements over many months and years of continuous outdoor exposure.
exampleZh: 通常称为 rust 的 Iron oxide 当 metallic iron 在 extended period of time 内 simultaneously exposed 于 oxygen 和 moisture 时形成 通过 a slow spontaneous electrochemical corrosion process 逐渐且 relentlessly converts the strong silvery-gray elemental metal 为 a weak flaky reddish-brown powdery substance 如果 destructive corrosive chemical reaction 被 allowed to continue unchecked and unprotected against the surrounding environmental elements over many months and years of continuous outdoor exposure 最终 crumbles away to nothing

exampleEn: Physicists use enormous particle accelerator facilities such as the Large Hadron Collider at CERN spanning several kilometers in underground circular tunnel diameter to smash subatomic particle together at velocities approaching the speed of light itself in order to briefly recreate the extreme conditions of unimaginably high energy and density that existed during the first tiny fraction of a second immediately following the Big Bang at the very birth of our universe some thirteen point eight billion years ago in the distant cosmic past.
exampleZh: 物理学家使用 enormous particle accelerator facilities 如 CERN 的 Large Hadron Collider spanning several kilometers in underground circular tunnel diameter 以接近光速的 velocities smash subatomic particle together 以 briefly recreate 在约十三点八亿年前在遥远宇宙过去宇宙诞生之初 Big Bang 后 immediately following 的 the very first tiny fraction of a second 期间存在的 extreme conditions of unimaginably high energy and density

exampleEn: The microbiologist carefully streaked a small sample of the unknown bacterial culture onto the surface of a sterile nutrient agar growth medium contained within a standard disposable plastic petri dish and then placed it upside down into a warm dark incubation chamber set to thirty-seven degrees Celsius to allow the individual bacterial cells to multiply and form visible isolated colonies over the following twenty-four to forty-eight hour growth and incubation period before further analysis could begin.
exampleZh: Microbiologist 将 unknown bacterial culture 的 small sample carefully streaked 到包含在 standard disposable plastic petri dish 中的 sterile nutrient agar growth medium 的表面上 然后将其 upside down placed into a warm dark incubation chamber set to thirty-seven degrees Celsius 以在 further analysis could begin 之前的 following twenty-four to forty-eight hour growth and incubation period 内 allow the individual bacterial cells to multiply and form visible isolated colonies

exampleEn: Water is a particularly remarkable chemical substance because it is one of the very few naturally occurring materials on Earth that can exist in all three common classical phase of matter namely solid ice liquid water and gaseous water vapor within the relatively narrow and easily accessible range of ambient temperature and atmospheric pressure conditions that prevail naturally across most of our planet's habitable surface environment throughout the changing seasons of the annual climatic cycle in temperate geographic regions of the world.
exampleZh: Water 是一种 particularly remarkable chemical substance 因为它是地球上 very few naturally occurring materials 之一 可以在 all three common classical phase of matter 即 solid ice liquid water 和 gaseous water vapor 中存在 在 naturally prevail across most of our planet's habitable surface environment throughout the changing seasons of the annual climatic cycle in temperate geographic regions of the world 的 relatively narrow and easily accessible range of ambient temperature and atmospheric pressure conditions 内

exampleEn: Plasma sometimes described as the fourth fundamental state of matter distinct from solids liquids and ordinary gases consists of an extremely hot electrically conductive ionized gas in which a significant fraction of the original neutral atoms or molecules have been stripped of one or more of their bound electrons creating a turbulent and highly dynamic electrically charged mixture of freely moving positively charged atomic ions and negatively charged liberated electrons that responds strongly and collectively to externally applied electromagnetic fields and forces in complex and often quite surprising ways.
exampleZh: 有时被描述为与 solids liquids 和 ordinary gases 不同的 fourth fundamental state of matter 的 plasma 由 extremely hot electrically conductive ionized gas consists 其中 a significant fraction of the original neutral atoms or molecules 已被 stripped of one or more of their bound electrons creating a turbulent and highly dynamic electrically charged mixture of freely moving positively charged atomic ions and negatively charged liberated electrons that responds strongly and collectively to externally applied electromagnetic fields and forces in complex and often quite surprising ways

exampleEn: Synthetic organic polymer such as polyethylene and polypropylene have become absolutely ubiquitous in modern daily life with global annual production now exceeding four hundred million metric tons because these versatile inexpensive lightweight and easily moldable plastic materials can be manufactured into an almost limitless variety of useful everyday consumer products from food packaging and beverage containers to clothing fibers vehicle components and sophisticated medical devices used in life-saving surgical procedures every single day.
exampleZh: Synthetic organic polymer 如 polyethylene 和 polypropylene 已成为 modern daily life 中 absolutely ubiquitous 全球 annual production 现在 exceeding four hundred million metric tons 因为这些 versatile inexpensive lightweight and easily moldable plastic materials 可以 manufactured into an almost limitless variety of useful everyday consumer products 从 food packaging 和 beverage containers 到 clothing fibers vehicle components 和 sophisticated medical devices used in life-saving surgical procedures every single day

exampleEn: When a solution of dissolved silver nitrate is carefully mixed with a solution of dissolved sodium chloride in a glass beaker under standard laboratory conditions a white solid precipitate of insoluble silver chloride immediately forms and gradually settles to the bottom of the container as a fine chalky powder demonstrating the classic double displacement chemical reaction between two soluble ionic salt compounds that produces an insoluble solid product that separates from the remaining clear liquid supernatant solution above it.
exampleZh: 当 dissolved silver nitrate 的溶液在 standard laboratory conditions 下在 glass beaker 中与 dissolved sodium chloride 的溶液 carefully mixed 时 a white solid precipitate of insoluble silver chloride immediately forms and gradually settles to the bottom of the container as a fine chalky powder demonstrated 两种 soluble ionic salt compounds 之间的 classic double displacement chemical reaction 产生一种 insoluble solid product that separates from the remaining clear liquid supernatant solution above it

exampleEn: The deep-space scientific probe traveled for over nine years through the cold vacuum of interplanetary space covering a total distance of more than five billion kilometers before finally reaching its intended destination at the distant dwarf planet Pluto and its system of five known moons located at the outermost fringes of our solar system where it captured the first detailed close-up images and scientific measurements ever obtained of this mysterious and previously unexplored frozen world.
exampleZh: Deep-space scientific probe 在 cold vacuum of interplanetary space 中 traveled for over nine years covering a total distance of more than five billion kilometers 然后 finally reaching its intended destination at the distant dwarf planet Pluto and its system of five known moons located at the outermost fringes of our solar system 在那里它 capture 了有史以来第一次获得的对这个神秘且 previously unexplored frozen world 的 detailed close-up images and scientific measurements

exampleEn: Protein are large complex biological macromolecules composed of long chains of smaller amino acid building blocks folded into precise and intricate three-dimensional shapes that determine their specific biological functions within living cells and organisms ranging from catalyzing metabolic chemical reactions as specialized enzymes to providing essential mechanical structural support to cells and tissues and transporting vital nutrient molecules throughout the complex internal circulatory systems of multicellular plants and animals across the natural world.
exampleZh: protein 是 large complex biological macromolecules composed of long chains of smaller amino acid building blocks folded into precise and intricate three-dimensional shapes that determine their specific biological functions within living cells and organisms  ranging from catalyzing metabolic chemical reactions as specialized enzymes 到 providing essential mechanical structural support to cells and tissues 和 transporting vital nutrient molecules throughout the complex internal circulatory systems of multicellular plants and animals across the natural world

exampleEn: The number of positively charged proton present in the atomic nucleus of an element determines its unique atomic number and therefore its fundamental chemical identity and position in the periodic table of the elements with hydrogen having just a single proton while uranium the heaviest naturally occurring element found in significant quantities on Earth contains exactly ninety-two protons densely packed together within its massive and inherently unstable radioactive atomic nucleus.
exampleZh: Positively charged proton present in an element 的 atomic nucleus 中的数量决定了其独特的 atomic number 以及其在 periodic table of the elements 中的 fundamental chemical identity and position  hydrogen 仅有一个 single proton 而 uranium  the heaviest naturally occurring element found in significant quantities on Earth 在其 massive and inherently unstable radioactive atomic nucleus 中 densely packed together exactly ninety-two protons

exampleEn: Quantum mechanics the fundamental physical theory that governs the behavior of matter and energy at the atomic and subatomic scale describes a strange and deeply counterintuitive microscopic world in which individual particles can exist in multiple different states simultaneously until measured and can instantaneously influence each other's properties across arbitrarily large distances through a mysterious phenomenon that Albert Einstein famously referred to as spooky action at a distance between entangled quantum particles separated by vast expanses of empty space and time.
exampleZh: Quantum mechanics the fundamental physical theory that governs the behavior of matter and energy at the atomic and subatomic scale 描述了一个 strange and deeply counterintuitive microscopic world 在这个世界中 individual particles can exist in multiple different states simultaneously until measured 并且可以通过 Albert Einstein famously referred to as spooky action at a distance 的 mysterious phenomenon across arbitrarily large distances instantaneously influence each other's properties between entangled quantum particles separated by vast expanses of empty space and time

exampleEn: Prolonged exposure to high levels of ionizing nuclear radiation can cause severe and potentially irreversible damage to the delicate DNA molecules within living cells leading to an increased lifetime risk of developing various types of cancer and other serious radiation-induced genetic illnesses which is why extreme caution and multiple layers of protective shielding are always required whenever anyone works in environments where significant sources of ionizing radioactive materials are present and being actively handled by trained professionals wearing specialized protective equipment.
exampleZh: Prolonged exposure 于 high levels of ionizing nuclear radiation 可对 living cells 内 delicate DNA molecules 造成 severe and potentially irreversible damage  leading to an increased lifetime risk of developing various types of cancer and other serious radiation-induced genetic illnesses 这就是为什么每当在 significant sources of ionizing radioactive materials are present and being actively handled by trained professionals wearing specialized protective equipment 的环境中工作时总是需要 extreme caution and multiple layers of protective shielding

exampleEn: Cosmic ray are highly energetic subatomic particles predominantly consisting of bare protons and heavier atomic nuclei that originate from distant astrophysical sources far beyond our own solar system such as the powerful shockwaves of ancient supernova explosions and the intense radiation beams emitted by rapidly rotating neutron stars and actively feeding supermassive black holes at the centers of remote galaxies scattered throughout the vast observable universe around us in every direction we can possibly look with our most sensitive and sophisticated astronomical instruments.
exampleZh: Cosmic ray 是 highly energetic subatomic particles predominantly consisting of bare protons and heavier atomic nuclei 它们 originate from distant astrophysical sources far beyond our own solar system 例如 ancient supernova explosions 的 powerful shockwaves 和 rapidly rotating neutron stars and actively feeding supermassive black holes at the centers of remote galaxies scattered throughout the vast observable universe around us in every direction we can possibly look with our most sensitive and sophisticated astronomical instruments 所 emit 的 intense radiation beams

exampleEn: The rate of a chemical reaction typically increases significantly with rising temperature because at higher temperatures a larger proportion of the colliding reactant molecules possess the minimum required activation energy necessary to break their existing chemical bonds and successfully rearrange into the new molecular configurations and bonding arrangements that characterize the desired final product compounds of that particular chemical transformation process being studied or utilized industrially.
exampleZh: Chemical reaction 的速率通常随温度升高而显著增加 因为在较高温度下 a larger proportion of the colliding reactant molecules possess the minimum required activation energy necessary to break their existing chemical bonds 并 successfully rearrange into the new molecular configurations and bonding arrangements that characterize the desired final product compounds of that particular chemical transformation process being studied or utilized industrially

exampleEn: Analytical chemists must always handle potentially hazardous laboratory reagent with extreme care and strict adherence to established safety protocols including wearing appropriate personal protective equipment such as chemical-resistant gloves and safety goggles and working only within properly functioning fume extraction hoods when dealing with volatile toxic or corrosive chemical substances that could pose significant immediate or long-term health risks to laboratory personnel if accidentally inhaled ingested or absorbed through unprotected skin contact in the workplace.
exampleZh: Analytical chemists 必须始终以 extreme care and strict adherence to established safety protocols handle potentially hazardous laboratory reagent 包括 wearing appropriate personal protective equipment such as chemical-resistant gloves and safety goggles 并在处理 volatile toxic or corrosive chemical substances 时仅 working only within properly functioning fume extraction hoods 这些物质如果 accidentally inhaled ingested or absorbed through unprotected skin contact in the workplace 可能对 laboratory personnel 构成 significant immediate or long-term health risks

exampleEn: Before any major scientific discovery can be widely accepted as valid and reliable by the broader international research community other independent laboratory groups at different institutions around the world must be able to successfully replicate the original experimental results under carefully controlled conditions producing substantially the same observed outcomes using the same published experimental procedures and analytical methods described in the original peer-reviewed scientific publication that first reported the novel and potentially important finding to the wider academic world.
exampleZh: 在任何 major scientific discovery 能被 broader international research community widely accepted as valid and reliable 之前世界各地的 different institutions 的 other independent laboratory groups 必须能够在 carefully controlled conditions 下 successfully replicate the original experimental results 使用 original peer-reviewed scientific publication that first reported the novel and potentially important finding to the wider academic world 中描述的 same published experimental procedures and analytical methods producing substantially the same observed outcomes

exampleEn: Certain species of bacteria have evolved the ability to resist the powerful antibiotic drugs that were previously capable of easily killing them through the gradual evolutionary accumulation over many successive bacterial generations of random genetic mutations that happen to confer even a very slight survival advantage to those particular individual bacterial cells carrying the protective altered genes when they are repeatedly exposed to the antibiotic compounds over extended periods of treatment time in hospital patients and agricultural livestock populations globally.
exampleZh: 某些 bacteria 的 species 已经 evolve 了 resist the powerful antibiotic drugs 的能力 这些药物以前 capable of easily killing them 通过在 many successive bacterial generations 中 gradual evolutionary accumulation of random genetic mutations that happen to confer even a very slight survival advantage to those particular individual bacterial cells carrying the protective altered genes 当它们在 hospital patients and agricultural livestock populations globally 中 over extended periods of treatment time repeatedly exposed 于 antibiotic compounds 时

exampleEn: Powerful supercomputers running highly sophisticated climate models can simulate the enormously complex interacting physical chemical and biological processes that govern the behavior of Earth's entire global atmosphere and ocean circulation systems over timescales spanning many decades and even centuries into the hypothetical future based on different plausible greenhouse gas emission scenarios and their projected warming impacts on worldwide average temperatures precipitation patterns and extreme weather event frequency and intensity across every major continental region on our rapidly changing warming planet.
exampleZh: 运行 highly sophisticated climate models 的 powerful supercomputers 可以 simulate 支配 Earth's entire global atmosphere and ocean circulation systems 行为的 enormously complex interacting physical chemical and biological processes 跨越 many decades and even centuries 的时间尺度进入 hypothetical future 基于 different plausible greenhouse gas emission scenarios and their projected warming impacts on worldwide average temperatures precipitation patterns and extreme weather event frequency and intensity across every major continental region on our rapidly changing warming planet

exampleEn: Vitamin C also known chemically as ascorbic acid is a water-soluble essential micronutrient that the human body cannot synthesize or store in significant amounts for extended periods of time and must therefore be regularly consumed through a balanced daily diet containing adequate quantities of fresh fruits and green leafy vegetables to prevent the development of the historically feared deficiency disease known as scurvy that once plagued long-distance sailors on extended ocean voyages lasting many months without access to fresh food supplies.
exampleZh: Vitamin C 化学上也称为 ascorbic acid 是一种 water-soluble essential micronutrient 人体不能 synthesize or store in significant amounts for extended periods of time 因此必须通过含有 adequate quantities of fresh fruits and green leafy vegetables 的 balanced daily diet regularly consumed 以 prevent 历史上 feared 的 deficiency disease known as scurvy 的 development 这种病 once plagued long-distance sailors on extended ocean voyages lasting many months without access to fresh food supplies

exampleEn: When ordinary white sunlight passes through a glass prism the different constituent wavelength components that together make up what we perceive as colorless white light are bent by slightly different amounts as they enter and exit the denser glass material causing them to spread out and separate spatially into the familiar continuous rainbow-like visible spectrum of distinct colors ranging from long-wavelength red at one end through orange yellow green and blue to short-wavelength violet at the opposite end of the observable visible light wavelength range detectable by the light-sensitive cone receptor cells in our human eyes.
exampleZh: 当普通白色阳光穿过 glass prism 时 共同构成我们 perceive as colorless white light 的不同 constituent wavelength components 在进入和离开 denser glass material 时被 slightly different amounts bent 导致它们 spread out and separate spatially into the familiar continuous rainbow-like visible spectrum of distinct colors 从一端的 long-wavelength red through orange yellow green and blue 到 opposite end of the observable visible light wavelength range 的 short-wavelength violet 可由我们人类眼睛中的 light-sensitive cone receptor cells detectable

exampleEn: Fungi reproduce by releasing enormous numbers of microscopic airborne spore into the surrounding environment that can be carried by wind currents over surprisingly large distances before eventually settling and if conditions of adequate moisture warmth and nutrient availability happen to be favorable germinating into new fungal colonies that will eventually in turn produce their own spore and continue the endless biological cycle of fungal reproduction and dispersal across the natural landscape from one generation to the next throughout the changing seasons of each passing year.
exampleZh: Fungi 通过向周围环境 releasing enormous numbers of microscopic airborne spore 来 reproduce 这些 spore 可以被 wind currents carried over surprisingly large distances 然后 eventually settling 如果 adequate moisture warmth and nutrient availability 的条件 happen to be favorable 就会 germinating into new fungal colonies 这些 colonies eventually 又 produce their own spore 并 continue the endless biological cycle of fungal reproduction and dispersal across the natural landscape from one generation to the next throughout the changing seasons of each passing year

exampleEn: Medical researchers are urgently working to develop a new effective vaccine specifically targeting the latest highly mutated and unusually contagious strain of the influenza virus that has recently emerged and is spreading rapidly through densely populated urban areas across multiple continents straining healthcare systems and causing widespread concern among international public health authorities and government pandemic preparedness agencies worldwide about the potential for a severe global disease outbreak in the coming winter respiratory illness season.
exampleZh: Medical researchers 正在 urgently working to develop a new effective vaccine specifically targeting the latest highly mutated and unusually contagious strain of the influenza virus that has recently emerged and is spreading rapidly through densely populated urban areas across multiple continents straining healthcare systems and causing widespread concern among international public health authorities and government pandemic preparedness agencies worldwide about the potential for a severe global disease outbreak in the coming winter respiratory illness season

exampleEn: The newly launched James Webb Space Telescope positioned nearly one point five million kilometers from Earth at a gravitationally stable point in space known as the second Sun-Earth Lagrange point can peer deep into the distant cosmos capturing faint infrared light from the very first primitive galaxies that began forming less than four hundred million years after the original Big Bang event that created our entire observable universe approximately thirteen point eight billion years ago in the unimaginably remote and ancient cosmic past.
exampleZh: Newly launched James Webb Space telescope positioned nearly one point five million kilometers from Earth at a gravitationally stable point in space known as the second Sun-Earth Lagrange point 可以 deep into the distant cosmos peer capturing faint infrared light from the very first primitive galaxies that began forming less than four hundred million years after the original Big Bang event that created our entire observable universe approximately thirteen point eight billion years ago in the unimaginably remote and ancient cosmic past

exampleEn: Before any new pharmaceutical drug can receive official regulatory approval for widespread clinical use in treating human patients it must first successfully pass through three sequential phases of rigorous randomized controlled clinical test involving increasingly large numbers of volunteer participants to convincingly demonstrate both its safety and its statistically meaningful medical efficacy compared to existing standard treatment options or a biologically inactive placebo control substance under carefully monitored and ethically supervised research conditions over a period of several years of systematic scientific investigation.
exampleZh: 在任何新 pharmaceutical drug 可以 receive official regulatory approval for widespread clinical use in treating human patients 之前它必须首先 successfully pass through three sequential phases of rigorous randomized controlled clinical test involving increasingly large numbers of volunteer participants 以 convincingly demonstrate 其 safety 和与 existing standard treatment options or a biologically inactive placebo control substance 相比的 statistically meaningful medical efficacy 在 carefully monitored and ethically supervised research conditions 下 over a period of several years of systematic scientific investigation 之后

exampleEn: A robust scientific theory is far more than just a casual guess or an unsupported hunch about how something in the natural world might possibly work it represents instead a comprehensive well-substantiated and repeatedly tested explanatory framework that has survived rigorous experimental scrutiny and successfully made accurate predictions about future observations across a wide range of related phenomena over an extended period of time earning broad acceptance within the relevant scientific community as the best currently available explanation for the observed facts and evidence at hand based on the cumulative weight of all the available data and logical reasoning supporting it.
exampleZh: 一个 robust scientific theory 远不止是 a casual guess or an unsupported hunch 关于自然世界中某事物可能如何运作 它代表的是一种 comprehensive well-substantiated and repeatedly tested explanatory framework 它 survived rigorous experimental scrutiny 并 successfully made accurate predictions about future observations across a wide range of related phenomena over an extended period of time earning broad acceptance within the relevant scientific community as the best currently available explanation for the observed facts and evidence at hand based on the cumulative weight of all the available data and logical reasoning supporting it

exampleEn: The pathologist carefully examined thin stained slices of the patient's biopsied liver tissue under a high-powered laboratory microscope searching for any telltale microscopic cellular abnormalities or distinctive patterns of structural tissue damage that might help the medical team definitively diagnose the underlying cause of the patient's persistent and unexplained chronic illness that had been troubling them for over six months without any clear explanation or effective treatment yet identified.
exampleZh: Pathologist 在 high-powered laboratory microscope 下 carefully examined thin stained slices of the patient's biopsied liver tissue searching for any telltale microscopic cellular abnormalities or distinctive patterns of structural tissue damage that might help the medical team definitively diagnose the underlying cause of the patient's persistent and unexplained chronic illness that had been troubling them for over six months without any clear explanation or effective treatment yet identified

exampleEn: The powerful electric motor in modern high-performance electric vehicles delivers maximum driving torque almost instantly from a complete standstill providing breathtaking acceleration from zero to one hundred kilometers per hour in under three seconds for the fastest production models currently available on the commercial automotive market which is a level of straight-line performance that even the most expensive and exotic gasoline-powered supercars from just a single generation ago could only dream of ever possibly achieving in their wildest engineering ambitions and performance fantasies.
exampleZh: Modern high-performance electric vehicles 中 powerful electric motor 从 complete standstill almost instantly delivers maximum driving torque 提供从零到一百公里每小时在不到三秒内的 breathtaking acceleration 对于目前 commercial automotive market 上可用的最快 production models 而言 这种 straight-line performance 水平是仅仅 a single generation ago 的 even the most expensive and exotic gasoline-powered supercars 在其 wildest engineering ambitions and performance fantasies 中也 only dream of ever possibly achieving 的

exampleEn: Phase three clinical trial are the final and most extensive stage of human testing required before a newly developed pharmaceutical drug or medical treatment can be submitted for formal evaluation and potential approval by government regulatory agencies and typically involve many thousands of volunteer participants recruited from multiple different hospitals and medical research centers spread across numerous countries and continents around the world to ensure that the collected data on safety and efficacy is statistically robust and broadly generalizable across diverse patient demographic populations and geographical regions.
exampleZh: Phase three clinical trial 是新开发的 pharmaceutical drug or medical treatment 在可以 submitted for formal evaluation and potential approval by government regulatory agencies 之前所需的 human testing 的 final and most extensive stage 通常涉及从 spread across numerous countries and continents around the world 的 multiple different hospitals and medical research centers recruited 数千名 volunteer participants 以确保收集的关于 safety 和 efficacy 的数据在统计上是 robust 的且 broadly generalizable across diverse patient demographic populations and geographical regions

exampleEn: Sound waves cannot propagate through the perfect vacuum of empty outer space because they require a material physical medium such as air water or solid metal consisting of atoms or molecules to mechanically transmit the traveling pattern of alternating compression and rarefaction regions from one physical location to another which is why the dramatic explosions and roaring engine sounds often depicted in science fiction movies set in the silent vastness of interstellar space are scientifically inaccurate and would in actual reality be completely inaudible and utterly silent to any nearby human observer or hypothetical alien listener positioned in the surrounding cosmic void.
exampleZh: Sound waves 不能通过 empty outer space 的 perfect vacuum propagate 因为它们需要 a material physical medium 如 air water or solid metal consisting of atoms or molecules 以 mechanically transmit the traveling pattern of alternating compression and rarefaction regions from one physical location to another 这就是为什么在 silent vastness of interstellar space 中设定的 science fiction movies 中经常 depict 的 dramatic explosions and roaring engine sounds 是 scientifically inaccurate 的 并且在 actual reality 中对于任何 nearby human observer or hypothetical alien listener positioned in the surrounding cosmic void 来说将是 completely inaudible and utterly silent 的

exampleEn: The escape velocity required for a spacecraft to permanently break free from the powerful gravitational pull of our planet Earth and journey onward into the vastness of interplanetary space without falling back down to the surface is approximately eleven point two kilometers per second or roughly forty thousand kilometers per hour which is over thirty times faster than the typical cruising speed of a commercial passenger jet airliner flying at maximum efficiency through the thin air at high altitude in the upper reaches of our planet's protective atmospheric layer surrounding the globe below.
exampleZh: Spacecraft 要 permanently break free from the powerful gravitational pull of our planet Earth and journey onward into the vastness of interplanetary space without falling back down to the surface 所需的 escape velocity 大约为 eleven point two kilometers per second 或 roughly forty thousand kilometers per hour 比 commercial passenger jet airliner at maximum efficiency through the thin air at high altitude in the upper reaches of our planet's protective atmospheric layer surrounding the globe below flying 的 typical cruising speed 快 over thirty times

exampleEn: Proper laboratory ventilation is absolutely critical for maintaining a safe and healthy working environment when handling volatile toxic or flammable chemical substances because the continuous flow of fresh filtered air through the workspace effectively removes and dilutes any potentially hazardous chemical vapors or fine airborne particulate matter that might otherwise gradually accumulate to dangerously high and potentially harmful airborne concentration levels within the enclosed and confined indoor space of the occupied laboratory room environment over the course of a busy working day.
exampleZh: 在处理 volatile toxic or flammable chemical substances 时 Proper laboratory ventilation 对于 maintaining a safe and healthy working environment absolutely critical 因为 fresh filtered air 的 continuous flow through the workspace effectively removes and dilutes 任何 potentially hazardous chemical vapors or fine airborne particulate matter 否则它们可能在 the course of a busy working day 中 gradually accumulate to dangerously high and potentially harmful airborne concentration levels 在 enclosed and confined indoor space of the occupied laboratory room environment 内

exampleEn: The electrical transmission grid carries high-voltage alternating current electricity at many hundreds of thousands of volts from distant centralized power generating stations through a nationwide network of thick metal conducting cables suspended high above the ground on tall steel lattice towers stretching across the countryside for vast distances before eventually being stepped down to much lower and safer household voltage levels by local substation transformers for final distribution into individual residential homes apartments schools and business premises throughout every city town and village across the entire country.
exampleZh: Electrical transmission grid 将 high-voltage alternating current electricity at many hundreds of thousands of volts 从 distant centralized power generating stations 通过悬挂在 tall steel lattice towers 上的 thick metal conducting cables 组成的 nationwide network carries 跨越 countryside 的 vast distances stretching 然后最终被 local substation transformers stepped down to much lower and safer household voltage levels 以最终 distribution 到个体 residential homes apartments schools and business premises throughout every city town and village across the entire country

exampleEn: According to the well-established physical principle known as Charles Law the volume of a fixed quantity of an ideal gas maintained at a constant pressure will expand predictably when the temperature of that gas is increased because the constituent gas molecules gain additional kinetic energy and move around more vigorously on average colliding more frequently and with greater force against the internal walls of their flexible expandable containing vessel thereby gradually pushing the movable boundaries of the container outward and increasing the total physical space that the heated expanding gas now occupies within the newly enlarged containing volume available to it.
exampleZh: 根据称为 Charles Law 的 well-established physical principle 保持在 constant pressure 下的 fixed quantity of an ideal gas 的 volume 将当该气体的温度升高时 predictably expand 因为 constituent gas molecules gain additional kinetic energy and move around more vigorously on average colliding more frequently and with greater force against the internal walls of their flexible expandable containing vessel  thereby gradually pushing the movable boundaries of the container outward 并 increasing the total physical space that the heated expanding gas now occupies within the newly enlarged containing volume available to it

exampleEn: A typical modern domestic LED light bulb consumes only about ten watt of electrical power to produce the same amount of visible illumination that would have previously required an old-fashioned incandescent filament bulb drawing approximately sixty watts from the household mains electricity supply representing an enormous improvement in household energy conversion efficiency of over eighty percent for the same usable light output delivered to the room being illuminated for reading and other everyday household tasks and activities.
exampleZh: 一个 typical modern domestic LED light bulb 仅 consume about ten watt 的 electrical power 即可产生与 old-fashioned incandescent filament bulb drawing approximately sixty watts from the household mains electricity supply  previously required 相同数量的可见照明 representing an enormous improvement in household energy conversion efficiency of over eighty percent for the same usable light output delivered to the room being illuminated for reading and other everyday household tasks and activities

exampleEn: The characteristic wavelength of visible electromagnetic radiation determines the specific color that the human eye and brain perceive when light of that particular wavelength enters the eye and stimulates the three different types of color-sensitive cone receptor cells densely packed across the central region of the light-sensitive retina at the back of the eyeball with shorter wavelengths around four hundred nanometers appearing as violet and blue while longer wavelengths approaching seven hundred nanometers appear as various shades of orange and deep red to the conscious visual perception system of the human observer viewing the world around them.
exampleZh: Visible electromagnetic radiation 的 characteristic wavelength 决定了当该特定 wavelength 的 light enters the eye and stimulates 三种不同类型的 color-sensitive cone receptor cells densely packed across the central region of the light-sensitive retina at the back of the eyeball 时人眼和大脑 perceive 的 specific color  shorter wavelengths around four hundred nanometers appearing as violet and blue 而 longer wavelengths approaching seven hundred nanometers 在 human observer viewing the world around them 的 conscious visual perception system 中 appear as various shades of orange and deep red

exampleEn: The new arterial road connecting the airport to the city center has reduced travel time by approximately forty percent for commuters.
exampleZh: 连接机场与市中心的新 arterial 道路将通勤者的旅行时间减少了约百分之四十

exampleEn: The tree-lined boulevard stretching through the heart of the historic district features dedicated bicycle lanes and wide pedestrian walkways on both sides of the street.
exampleZh: 穿过历史区中心的 tree-lined boulevard 在街道两侧设有专用自行车道和宽阔的 pedestrian walkways

exampleEn: The once quiet fishing village has been transformed into a bustling tourist destination attracting over two million visitors annually to its colorful waterfront markets.
exampleZh: 曾经安静的渔村已变成 bustling 旅游 destination 每年吸引超过两百万 visitors 到其色彩缤纷的海滨市场

exampleEn: The newly constructed highway bypass diverts heavy truck traffic away from the narrow streets of the historic town center preserving its quiet residential character.
exampleZh: 新建的高速公路 bypass 将重型卡车 traffic 从 historic town center 的狭窄街道 divert 开 保护了其安静的 residential character

exampleEn: Ride-hailing applications have largely replaced traditional street-hailed cab services in most major cities around the world over the past decade of rapid technological and social change.
exampleZh: 在过去十年 rapid technological and social change 中 Ride-hailing applications 已 largely replaced 世界上大多数主要城市的传统街头 hailed cab services

exampleEn: The first-class carriage on the high-speed intercity express train features spacious reclining seats individual power outlets and complimentary wireless internet for all passengers traveling between major destinations.
exampleZh: 城际高速 express train 上的头等 carriage 设有宽敞的 reclining seats 个人 power outlets 和 complimentary wireless internet 供所有在主要 destinations 之间 traveling 的乘客使用

exampleEn: Security personnel at the airport checkpoint carefully inspected all luggage using advanced x-ray scanning equipment before allowing any passengers to proceed onward to the departure gates.
exampleZh: Airport checkpoint 的安全人员使用 advanced x-ray scanning equipment 在允许任何乘客 proceed onward to the departure gates 之前 carefully inspected 了所有行李

exampleEn: The local government has introduced significant subsidies for electric bicycles to encourage more commuter to switch from driving private cars to cleaner modes of daily urban transportation.
exampleZh: Local government 已引入 significant subsidies for electric bicycles 以 encourage 更多 commuter 从 driving private cars switch 到更清洁的 daily urban transportation 模式

exampleEn: The new pedestrian crossing equipped with countdown timers and audible signals has made the busy intersection considerably safer for elderly residents and schoolchildren alike each morning and afternoon.
exampleZh: 配备 countdown timers 和 audible signals 的新 pedestrian crossing 使繁忙的 intersection 对老年居民和学童 alike each morning and afternoon considerably safer

exampleEn: The city has installed hundreds of electric vehicle charging stations along the curbside of residential streets making it more convenient for apartment dwellers who lack private garages to own plug-in cars.
exampleZh: 该市在 residential streets 的 curbside 安装了数百个 electric vehicle charging stations 使缺乏 private garages 的 apartment dwellers 更方便拥有 plug-in cars

exampleEn: Dedicated protected bicycle lanes separated from motor vehicle traffic by concrete barriers have significantly reduced serious collisions between cyclist and cars in the downtown urban core area.
exampleZh: 由 concrete barriers 与 motor vehicle traffic separated 的 dedicated protected bicycle lanes 已 significantly reduced 市中心 urban core area 中 cyclist 与 cars 之间的 serious collisions

exampleEn: The regional bus depot serves as a central maintenance and overnight parking facility for over two hundred public transit vehicles operating across the entire metropolitan county area daily.
exampleZh: Regional bus depot 充当 central maintenance and overnight parking facility 服务于 daily operating across the entire metropolitan county area 的两百多辆 public transit vehicles

exampleEn: Drivers were advised to take a lengthy detour around the collapsed bridge while emergency repair crews worked continuously around the clock for three weeks to restore the essential cross-river transportation link.
exampleZh: Drivers 被 advised to take a lengthy detour around the collapsed bridge 而 emergency repair crews 在 three weeks 里 continuously worked around the clock 以 restore the essential cross-river transportation link

exampleEn: The downtown revitalization project aims to attract new businesses restaurants and cultural venues to the historic city center while preserving its unique architectural heritage and traditional walkable character.
exampleZh: downtown revitalization project 旨在 attract new businesses restaurants and cultural venues 到 historic city center 同时 preserving its unique architectural heritage and traditional walkable character

exampleEn: The reinforced concrete embankment along the river through the downtown district was designed to withstand a once-in-a-century flood event protecting billions of dollars worth of riverside real estate and critical infrastructure.
exampleZh: 穿过 downtown district 的河流沿岸的 reinforced concrete embankment 被设计为 withstand a once-in-a-century flood event 保护价值数十亿美元的 riverside real estate and critical infrastructure

exampleEn: The deep underground metro station is equipped with four long escalator that transport passengers efficiently from the train platform level up to the street surface entrance nearly forty meters directly above them.
exampleZh: Deep underground metro station 配备了四部 long escalator 将 passengers efficiently transport 从 train platform level 直到其正上方 nearly forty meters 的 street surface entrance

exampleEn: The new airport express train service connects the international terminal directly with the main downtown central railway station in just eighteen minutes with no intermediate stops whatsoever along the entire dedicated transit route.
exampleZh: New airport express train service 将 international terminal 与 main downtown central railway station directly connects 仅需十八分钟 整条 dedicated transit route 上没有任何 intermediate stops whatsoever

exampleEn: Public transportation fare have been frozen for the third consecutive year as part of the municipal government's ongoing efforts to ease the increasing financial burden on low-income daily commuters across the metropolitan region.
exampleZh: 作为 municipal government 的 ongoing efforts to ease the increasing financial burden on low-income daily commuters across the metropolitan region 的一部分 Public transportation fare 已连续第三年冻结

exampleEn: The passenger ferry service between the mainland port and the two largest offshore islands carries over fifteen thousand travelers and local residents across the busy harbor waterway each and every single day of the year.
exampleZh: 大陆港口与两个最大 offshore islands 之间的 passenger ferry service 每年 each and every single day carries over fifteen thousand travelers and local residents 横跨繁忙的 harbor waterway

exampleEn: The newly constructed concrete flyover eliminates the need for vehicles on the major cross-country highway to stop at the previously dangerous and congested at-grade intersection with the busy local ring road encircling the entire metropolitan perimeter.
exampleZh: 新建的 concrete flyover eliminates vehicles on the major cross-country highway 在 previously dangerous and congested at-grade intersection with the busy local ring road encircling the entire metropolitan perimeter 停车的需要

exampleEn: The government has announced major new investments to shift a much larger proportion of long-distance domestic freight transportation from heavily polluting diesel trucks onto cleaner and more energy-efficient electrified railway corridors across the nation.
exampleZh: 政府已宣布 major new investments 以将更大比例的 long-distance domestic freight transportation 从 heavily polluting diesel trucks shift 到全国 cleaner and more energy-efficient electrified railway corridors 上

exampleEn: The apartment building's secure underground parking garage can accommodate up to three hundred vehicles across two levels with dedicated charging stations for electric cars located near the main entrance ramp on each floor.
exampleZh: 公寓楼的 secure underground parking garage 可在两层 accommodate up to three hundred vehicles 每层 main entrance ramp 附近设有 dedicated charging stations for electric cars

exampleEn: Severe traffic gridlock paralyzed the entire downtown business district for over four hours after a major accident involving a tanker truck blocked all three southbound lanes of the central expressway during the busy evening rush hour.
exampleZh: 在一辆 tanker truck 在繁忙的 evening rush hour 期间 blocked all three southbound lanes of the central expressway 的 major accident 之后 Severe traffic gridlock paralyzed 整个 downtown business district over four hours

exampleEn: The natural deep-water harbor has been expanded through a massive land reclamation and dredging project that now allows the port to accommodate the largest modern container ships currently sailing the world's major ocean trade routes.
exampleZh: Natural deep-water harbor 已通过 massive land reclamation and dredging project expanded 现在允许港口 accommodate currently sailing the world's major ocean trade routes 的最大的 modern container ships

exampleEn: The newly completed coastal highway winds through some of the most spectacular and previously inaccessible scenery in the entire country opening up remote fishing villages and pristine beaches to tourism development.
exampleZh: 新完成的沿海 highway winds through 全国一些最壮观且 previously inaccessible 的 scenery opening up remote fishing villages and pristine beaches to tourism development

exampleEn: In many remote rural areas with extremely limited public transportation options local residents still regularly hitchhike to reach the nearest town for essential supplies and medical appointments several times each month.
exampleZh: 在许多 public transportation options extremely limited 的 remote rural areas 当地居民仍然 regularly hitchhike 以 reach the nearest town for essential supplies and medical appointments 每月 several times

exampleEn: Excessive use of vehicle horn in residential neighborhoods after ten o'clock at night has been banned by the municipal authorities with substantial fines imposed for any verified violation of this recently enacted noise control ordinance.
exampleZh: 在 residential neighborhoods 中晚上十点后 Excessive use of vehicle horn 已被 municipal authorities banned 对任何 verified violation of this recently enacted noise control ordinance 处以 substantial fines

exampleEn: The redesigned intersection now features dedicated left-turn signal phases and clearly marked bicycle crossing zones that have reduced the annual number of reported traffic accidents at this historically dangerous urban junction by over sixty percent.
exampleZh: Redesigned intersection 现在设有 dedicated left-turn signal phases 和 clearly marked bicycle crossing zones 已使这个 historically dangerous urban junction 的年 reported traffic accidents 数量减少 over sixty percent

exampleEn: The railway junction where three major intercity train lines meet serves as a vital strategic transfer point for both passenger services and industrial freight operations connecting the northern and southern regions of the country together.
exampleZh: 三条主要城际 train lines 交汇的 railway junction 充当 vital strategic transfer point for both passenger services and industrial freight operations connecting the northern and southern regions of the country together

exampleEn: Delivery drivers are permitted to park briefly along the yellow kerb for up to twenty minutes while actively loading or unloading heavy goods and packages to nearby shops and commercial business premises in the designated urban loading zone areas.
exampleZh: Delivery drivers 被允许在 yellow kerb 沿线 briefly park 最多二十分钟 同时在 designated urban loading zone areas 中 actively loading or unloading heavy goods and packages 到附近的 shops and commercial business premises

exampleEn: The city has converted one traffic lane on each side of the major downtown avenue into a dedicated bus-only corridor during peak commuting hours significantly improving public transit speed and reliability for thousands of daily passengers.
exampleZh: 该市已将 major downtown avenue 每侧的一个 traffic lane 在 peak commuting hours 期间 convert 为 dedicated bus-only corridor significantly improving public transit speed and reliability for thousands of daily passengers

exampleEn: All drivers over the age of seventy are required to renew their driving license annually and must pass a comprehensive medical examination including both vision and cognitive function tests to ensure they remain fit to operate motor vehicles safely on public roads.
exampleZh: 所有七十岁以上的 drivers 需要 annually renew their driving license 并且必须 pass a comprehensive medical examination including both vision and cognitive function tests 以确保他们仍然 fit to operate motor vehicles safely on public roads

exampleEn: The powerful new electric locomotive can pull freight trains weighing over ten thousand metric tons while consuming sixty percent less energy than the aging diesel-electric models it was specifically designed and built to replace on the national railway network.
exampleZh: Powerful new electric locomotive 可以 pull freight trains weighing over ten thousand metric tons 同时 consuming 比它 specifically designed and built to replace on the national railway network 的 aging diesel-electric models 少 sixty percent less energy

exampleEn: Airport staff carefully weigh and tag each piece of checked luggage before it disappears behind the rubber curtains onto the complex automated conveyor belt sorting system that directs every bag to its correct departing flight gate.
exampleZh: Airport staff 在每件 checked luggage disappears behind the rubber curtains onto the complex automated conveyor belt sorting system that directs every bag to its correct departing flight gate 之前 carefully weigh and tag 它

exampleEn: The city's expanding metro network now serves over five million passengers daily across twelve color-coded lines and one hundred and eighty-seven stations spread throughout the greater metropolitan area and its densely populated surrounding suburban districts and satellite commuter towns.
exampleZh: 该市 expanding metro network 现在每天服务于 over five million passengers 跨越 twelve color-coded lines 和 one hundred and eighty-seven stations 分布在整个 greater metropolitan area 及其 densely populated surrounding suburban districts and satellite commuter towns

exampleEn: Hybrid vehicles typically achieve significantly better fuel mileage in stop-and-go city traffic conditions than on highways because their regenerative braking systems can recover much of the energy normally wasted as heat during frequent urban deceleration and braking events.

exampleZh: 晚上七点到十点 prime time television viewing hours 期间的 Advertising rates 通常比 broadcast day 中的 any other time slot 高三到四倍 reflecting the much larger potential audience reach available during those peak evening viewing hours

exampleEn: Despite confident predictions of its imminent demise in the digital age the print publishing industry has shown remarkable resilience adapting its business models by focusing on high-quality long-form journalism and premium subscription services that sophisticated readers are willing to pay for directly.
exampleZh: Despite confident predictions of its imminent demise in the digital age print publishing industry 已显示 remarkable resilience adapting its business models by focusing on high-quality long-form journalism and premium subscription services that sophisticated readers are willing to pay for directly

exampleEn: The executive producer is responsible for overseeing every creative and financial aspect of the television series from the initial script development through to casting filming and the final post-production editing and sound mixing processes before the completed episodes are delivered to the network for scheduled broadcast transmission.
exampleZh: Executive producer 负责 overseeing every creative and financial aspect of the television series 从 initial script development 到 casting filming 以及在 completed episodes are delivered to the network for scheduled broadcast transmission 之前的 final post-production editing and sound mixing processes

exampleEn: The magazine published a revealing in-depth profile of the famously reclusive billionaire philanthropist based on over twenty hours of unprecedented face-to-face interviews conducted at his private island retreat over the course of three extended weekend visits spanning nearly six months.
exampleZh: 该 magazine published a revealing in-depth profile of the famously reclusive billionaire philanthropist based on over twenty hours of unprecedented face-to-face interviews conducted at his private island retreat over the course of three extended weekend visits spanning nearly six months

exampleEn: The celebrity hired a new publicist to help repair her damaged public image following a series of embarrassing personal scandals that had dominated the tabloid headlines for weeks and seriously threatened her lucrative endorsement contracts with several major international luxury fashion and cosmetics brands.
exampleZh: 这位 celebrity hired a new publicist 帮助 repair her damaged public image following a series of embarrassing personal scandals that had dominated the tabloid headlines for weeks and seriously threatened her lucrative endorsement contracts with several major international luxury fashion and cosmetics brands

exampleEn: Academic researchers are under enormous professional pressure to regularly publish their findings in high-impact peer-reviewed journals because their career advancement funding opportunities and professional reputation depend heavily on their track record of successfully producing and disseminating influential published work.
exampleZh: Academic researchers 处于 enormous professional pressure 下 regularly publish their findings in high-impact peer-reviewed journals 因为他们的 career advancement funding opportunities and professional reputation depend heavily on their track record of successfully producing and disseminating influential published work

exampleEn: The researchers distributed a detailed forty-item questionnaire to over two thousand randomly selected participants across the country to gather statistically meaningful data about changing public attitudes toward social media use and government regulation of online content platforms.
exampleZh: Researchers distributed a detailed forty-item questionnaire to over two thousand randomly selected participants across the country 以 gather statistically meaningful data about changing public attitudes toward social media use and government regulation of online content platforms

exampleEn: Despite the proliferation of digital streaming services and podcast platforms traditional radio broadcasting still reaches over eighty percent of the adult population each week making it one of the most enduring and resilient forms of mass communication ever invented and adopted by modern human civilization globally.
exampleZh: 尽管 digital streaming services and podcast platforms 的 proliferation traditional radio broadcasting 仍然每周 reaches over eighty percent of the adult population 使其成为 modern human civilization globally 有史以来 invented and adopted 的最 enduring and resilient forms of mass communication 之一

exampleEn: Television network executives obsessively monitor the overnight viewer rating for each primetime program because even a small decline in audience share can translate into millions of dollars in lost advertising revenue over the course of a single television season broadcast schedule year.
exampleZh: Television network executives obsessively monitor the overnight viewer rating for each primetime program 因为即使是 audience share 的 small decline 也可能在 single television season broadcast schedule year 的 course 中 translate into millions of dollars in lost advertising revenue
